notes: Primary image (img-0.jpeg): Final plated dish (pork belly & ume-shiso roll).
Step-by-step images (img-1 to img-6.jpeg): Ingredients, sauce prep, shiso cutting, umeboshi paste, meat layering, and rolling process.

Context: A user-shared recipe for *Ume-Shiso Pork Belly Rolls*—a tangy, refreshing twist on classic teriyaki-glazed meat rolls, ideal for bento lunches or quick dinners, posted on a Japanese home-cooking community platform.

Content.title: Easy Tonight’s Dinner! Pork Belly & Ume-Shiso Meat Rolls (Bento-Friendly)
Content.description: This dish elevates classic sweet-savory teriyaki glaze with the bright acidity of pickled umeboshi (plum) and fresh shiso leaves, creating a refreshing, appetite-stimulating pork roll. The bold flavors make it perfect for lunch, dinner, or as a *rice-thief* (go-han-doro) bento item that pairs wonderfully with steamed rice.

instructions.text: Gather all ingredients. Combine liquid seasonings (▶) in a bowl.
Trim shiso stems and cut leaves in half lengthwise.
Remove umeboshi pits, mash the flesh into a paste.
Layer 2 pork belly slices slightly offset. Place ½ tsp umeboshi paste and a shiso half on the meat, then roll tightly. Repeat.
Lightly coat rolls in potato starch. Pan-fry in oil over medium heat until browned, then add the sauce to glaze.
Slice into bite-sized pieces and serve with rice or pack in a bento.
